---
title: "Reputational Pressure Pilot Data Analysis"
author: "Kaelin Main"
date: "2026-07-14"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r libraries}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
```


```{r read-data}
data <- read_excel("pilot_data/rep_pressure_deidentified.xlsx")
```

```{r clean-data}
data <- data %>% 
  mutate(condition_binary = if_else(condition == "high rep pressure", 1, 0)) %>%
  mutate(trialfirst_binary = if_else(`trial first` == "high", 1, 0)) %>% 
  rename(choice = `choice combination code`) %>% 
  mutate(choice = as.factor(choice))
```

```{r plot}
  ggplot(data, aes(fill = choice, x = condition)) +
    geom_bar(position = "fill", width = 0.6) +
    labs(x = "Condition",
         y = " ") +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("High reputational pressure", "Low reputational pressure")) +
    scale_fill_discrete(
      labels = c("Book for both questions",
                 "Toy for both questions",
                 "Book for low certainty, toy for high certainty",
                 "Toy for low certainty, book for high certainty"),
      name = "Choice"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  ggplot(data, aes(fill = choice, x = condition)) +
    geom_bar(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
    labs(x = "Condition",
         y = "Count") +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("High reputational pressure", "Low reputational pressure")) +
    scale_fill_discrete(
      labels = c("Book for both questions",
                 "Toy for both questions",
                 "Book for low certainty, toy for high certainty",
                 "Toy for low certainty, book for high certainty"),
      name = "Choice"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

```{r plot-wrapped}

data <- data %>% 
  mutate(trialfirst_binary = as.factor(trialfirst_binary)) %>%
  mutate(condition_binary = as.factor(condition_binary))

  ggplot(data, aes(x = trialfirst_binary, fill = choice)) +
    geom_bar(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) + 
    facet_wrap(~condition_binary, labeller = as_labeller(c(
      "0" = "Low reputational pressure",
      "1" = "High reputational pressure"
    ))) +
    labs(x = "Trial order",
         y = "Count") +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Low cert first", "High cert first")) +
    scale_fill_discrete(
      labels = c("Book for both questions",
                 "Toy for both questions",
                 "Book for low certainty, toy for high certainty",
                 "Toy for low certainty, book for high certainty"),
      name = "Choice"
    ) 
    

```
